title: Electrician
description: Install, maintain, and repair electrical systems in homes, commercial buildings, and industrial plants. SA's energy crisis has made qualified electricians more valuable than ever.

employability.demand: Very high — artisan shortage is acute; on Critical Skills List; loadshedding-era demand spike.
employability.outlook: Exceptional; solar installation, EV charging infrastructure, and grid maintenance all growing.
pathways: N1–N6 at TVET college + electrician's learnership/apprenticeship (2–4 years)
Trade test at QCTO
Contractors, municipalities, Eskom, solar companies, self-employed
employability.employment_note: The primary pathway is TVET college (N1–N6) followed by a registered apprenticeship and trade test — not a university degree. University degrees exist in Electrical Engineering for those who want to go the professional engineering route.

title: Millwright / Fitter & Turner
description: Install, maintain, and repair industrial machinery and mechanical equipment. Millwrights are multi-skilled artisans who keep factories, mines, and plants running.

employability.demand: Very high — listed as one of the most in-demand artisan trades in SA.
employability.outlook: Strong; automation and manufacturing expansion keeping demand elevated.
pathways: N1–N6 Mechanical Engineering at TVET + apprenticeship (2–4 years)
QCTO trade test
Mining companies, paper mills, chemical plants, food manufacturers
employability.employment_note: Primary pathway is TVET college followed by a registered apprenticeship and trade test. University mechanical engineering degree is for those targeting ECSA-registered professional engineering.

title: Boilermaker / Welder
description: Fabricate, assemble, and repair metal structures — pressure vessels, tanks, pipelines, and structural steel. Work in construction, energy, mining, and ship repair.

employability.demand: High — listed on Critical Skills List; fabrication skills needed across heavy industry.
employability.outlook: Strong; infrastructure projects and energy sector driving demand.
pathways: N1–N6 at TVET + boilermaker apprenticeship (2–4 years)
QCTO trade test
Construction, mining, energy, ship repair, structural steel companies
employability.employment_note: TVET college and apprenticeship is the standard route. No university degree is required to become a boilermaker.

title: Plumber
description: Install and repair pipes, fixtures, and drainage systems in residential, commercial, and industrial settings. Water infrastructure demand in SA keeps plumbers in steady work.

employability.demand: High — plumbing shortage especially in residential and municipal maintenance.
employability.outlook: Stable; water conservation retrofits and infrastructure repair generating demand.
pathways: N1–N6 Civil/Plumbing at TVET + plumbing apprenticeship (2–4 years)
PIRB registration after trade test
Contractors, municipalities, property management, self-employed
employability.employment_note: TVET college and registered apprenticeship is the route. Plumbers must register with PIRB after their trade test.

title: Diesel / Auto Mechanic
description: Service, diagnose, and repair diesel engines in trucks, buses, construction equipment, and agricultural machinery — or petrol vehicles and light commercial vehicles as an auto mechanic.

employability.demand: High — transport sector depends on mechanics; fleet operators in short supply.
employability.outlook: Stable; EV transition creating demand for upskilling but diesel dominant for a decade.
pathways: N1–N6 Diesel / Motor Mechanics at TVET + apprenticeship (2–4 years)
QCTO trade test
Transport companies, OEM dealers, fleet operators, mines
employability.employment_note: TVET college and apprenticeship is the standard route. OEM dealerships (Mercedes, Scania, Volvo) run their own learnerships aligned to TVET.

title: Instrumentation Technician
description: Install, calibrate, and maintain measurement and control instruments in industrial plants — flow meters, pressure gauges, control valves, and SCADA systems. Critical in petrochemical, food, and mining plants.

employability.demand: Very high — one of the most acute artisan shortages in SA's industrial sector.
employability.outlook: Exceptional; process automation and industrial IoT amplifying demand.
pathways: N1–N6 Electrical / Instrumentation at TVET + learnership (2–4 years)
QCTO trade test
Refineries, chemical plants, food manufacturers, mining
employability.employment_note: Instrumentation is a specialist artisan trade — TVET + apprenticeship is the route. The skill is also available via a National Diploma in Electrical Engineering (Instrumentation) at some universities.

title: Carpenter / Joiner
description: Cut, shape, and install timber and panel products — from roof trusses and door frames to fitted furniture and shopfitting. One of the most entry-accessible skilled trades.

employability.demand: Moderate-high — construction sector always needs carpenters.
employability.outlook: Stable; housing delivery programmes providing consistent work.
pathways: N1–N4 Carpentry / Woodwork at TVET + apprenticeship (2–4 years)
QCTO trade test
Construction companies, shopfitters, furniture manufacturers, self-employed
employability.employment_note: Apprenticeship through a TVET college and registered employer is the route. No university degree required.
